comment: Per-scenario regression config for tests/Aws2Azure.PerfTests. Two gates: (1) ABSOLUTE floors/ceilings in 'scenarios' (minThroughputPerSec / maxP99Ms), enforced per-test by PerfResult.AssertNoRegression — either field 0 opts out of that half; kept as wide catastrophe-detectors on emulator-bound paths whose azure-sdk.* baseline is absent OR too unstable to be a ruler (the Service Bus emulator's own SDK send p50 swings 3-5x run-to-run because it link-attaches per send while the proxy pools connections, so sqs.SendMessage / sns.Publish are gated on a THROUGHPUT FLOOR ONLY (their p99 is bimodal on the proxy side too — a rare cold AMQP link-attach lands a multi-second spike in the top 1% unpredictably, observed 234 ms one run and 3108 ms the next — so any absolute p99 ceiling flaps just like the relative gate; the throughput floor plus AssertHealthy is the only stable catastrophe signal)). dynamodb.BatchGetItem is likewise absolute-gated (maxP99Ms 700) not paired: there the PROXY p99 is the stable side (~240-340 ms, unimodal Cosmos REST) while the azure-sdk.Cosmos.ReadManyItems baseline is the jittery, structurally-unfair ruler (a specialized batched SDK API the proxy implements as N point-reads; its p99 collapsed 106->45 ms one run, spiking the ratio to 5.3x with no proxy change). (2) RELATIVE proxy-vs-baseline ratios in 'pairings', enforced once per run by RelativeRegressionGateTests (Category=RelativeGate) reading docs/perf/baseline-latest.json. The relative gate is the meaningful signal where the baseline is STABLE: emulator tail-latency jitter hits BOTH the proxy and its azure-sdk.* baseline, so the ratio cancels the noise and fails only when the PROXY adds overhead. METRIC BY SHAPE: REST + AMQP-receive pairs gate on p99-ratio (unimodal, stable baseline); the EventHubs send pair (kinesis.PutRecord) gates on p50-ratio (EH baseline is stable at 3-5ms; p99 would be cold-link-attach noise). A 0 ratio opts out of that dimension. azure-sdk.* baselines are reference rulers — {0,0} absolute, never fail the build. Operators bump these deliberately when a proxy change is expected to move performance. EVERY scenario must have a 'scenarios' entry — KnownPerfScenariosTests asserts coverage. (3) UNDER-LOAD MEMORY (#274): optional per-scenario 'maxPeakWorkingSetMb' (proxy process peak resident set over the measure window) and 'maxAllocBytesPerOp' (mean managed bytes allocated per completed op) ceilings, enforced by PerfResult.AssertNoRegression only when the run actually measured memory (the proxy self-reports via aws2azure_process_working_set_bytes / aws2azure_dotnet_gc_* gauges on /_aws2azure/metrics, scraped by ProxyMemoryProbe). 0 / absent opts out — high-risk scenarios (DynamoDb Scan/Query/Batch, SQS/SNS publish, S3 put/get) carry GENEROUS catastrophe ceilings seeded from the first CI perf run (run 27382199456): maxAllocBytesPerOp ~2.5-3x the observed per-op churn and a wide 400 MB working-set ceiling (vs ~100-154 MB observed) — sized to trip a real regression (e.g. a per-op allocation doubling or a pooling regression / leak) without flapping on run-to-run jitter. Tighten deliberately as more runs accumulate, exactly like the throughput floors above. NOTE peak working set is a cumulative high-water mark across all scenarios sharing one proxy process — maxAllocBytesPerOp is the scenario-attributable churn signal.
